page: Amazon Web Services c8i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core and text processing capabilities. The c8i.2xlarge achieves a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2207.0 and a text processing score of 2990.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Single-threaded gzip compression at 34.52 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.45 GB/sec rank in the top 25%. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) support efficient execution, delivering a strong 291.25 tokens per second for text generation using a small 135M parameter LLM. Other multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database benchmarks perform in the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.2xlarge offers a high-density compute profile with DDR5 memory and dedicated CPU allocation, making it a viable option for compute-bound tasks that do not require local NVMe storage. The lack of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale network-attached storage independently. This instance is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads such as single-threaded data processing, text compilation, web serving, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs across 4 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It includes 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 4 Gbps baseline network speed. The instance does not bundle local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2207.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds. It also features Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which assist in small-scale machine learning inference. This instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads, text processing, and web serving where dedicated CPU resources are required without the need for local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and top-tier single-core performance for dedicated processing workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated workloads
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for compute-intensive tasks, web serving, and text processing
